critical_issues: Discrepancy in customer's name between transcript and MER.
Discrepancy in customer's current location between transcript and MER.
MER incorrectly records 'No' for triglycerides despite customer disclosing 'mild' and elevated levels.
MER incorrectly records 'No' for eye problems despite customer disclosing 'left eye sees a little blurry' (later confirmed as cataract).
MER incorrectly records 'No' for thyroid disorders despite customer disclosing 'Thyroidism, it's about six, six and a half'.
MER incorrectly records 'No' for abnormal findings in recent tests despite customer disclosing 'slight variations' in triglycerides.
recommendations: Doctor should confirm and accurately record the customer's full name as per policy documents.
Doctor should confirm and accurately record the customer's current location.
Doctor should ensure all disclosed medical conditions, even if mild or self-managed, are accurately reflected in the MER.
Doctor should clarify the frequency and units of alcohol consumption to ensure accurate recording in MER.
Doctor should explicitly ask about annual health checkups and their findings, rather than inferring or marking 'Yes' when the customer states 'no other problems' for general health questions.

customer_satisfaction_indicators: Customer is cooperative and provides detailed answers.
Customer expresses trust in the doctor's understanding ('You also would have understood. Whether I'm telling the truth or lying, it is clearly understood.').
Customer is patient during pauses ('Just a minute, sir, you take your own time.').

validation_errors: Customer's name in transcript (Kolapudi Eswara Vijayakumar) differs from MER (Kolapudi Victor Vijaya Vara Kumar).
Customer's location in transcript (Kurnool) differs from MER (KHAMAM,TELANGANA,INDIA).
Alcohol consumption frequency in transcript (1-3 times a month) differs from MER (1 unit per week).
Customer disclosed mild triglycerides (180-190) and thyroidism (6-6.5), but MER recorded 'No' for these conditions and 'No' for abnormal findings in tests.
